repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 — generic non-Gaussian cumulant direction recovery, not Gaussian score-Jacobian local geometry

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score — population SCORE leaf characterization, without a C_m-to-Chernoff expansion
chernoff1952measure — generic simple-versus-simple exponent; the mirror C_m indexing depends on Theorem 1
chernoff1952measure — standard likelihood-ratio local/fixed-alternative machinery; the causal mirror specialization is new
zhu2023sample — SCORE recovery bound, not this conditional centered cross-fitted diagonal-Jacobian contrast
koehler2023efficiency — score-matching versus likelihood efficiency for density estimation, not tilted causal-order losses for this template

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Proposition 2 / ass:jacobian-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The L4 rate for a common hypothesis-independent nuisance-training map is maintained rather than derived from primitive trainer conditions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The score-contrast sign-consistency conclusion is accepted only conditional on ass:jacobian-estimability, n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, and r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))); no exponent or concrete-trainer guarantee is implied.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Give a worked smooth mechanism, such as a normalized quadratic Hermite perturbation, and explicitly compute c_1, c_2, C_m, and the resulting local error curve to make the information calibration inspectable.

Replace the maintained nuisance-rate premise with primitive sufficient conditions for one concrete score-Jacobian trainer, at least for a bounded-smooth subclass, and verify the tilted-loss criterion rather than only sign consistency.
Extend the mirror calculation to a nontrivial normalized tangent class and state a uniform lower/upper comparison, so the result is no longer only a single least-favorable path.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 on the field-tier mirror-path kernel, retaining the nuisance-estimation result explicitly as a conditional secondary rung.
